skill.name: product-manager-toolkit
skill.description: Product management toolkit for prioritizing features, analyzing customer interviews, drafting PRDs, and structuring discovery and go-to-market work.
skill.summary: Prioritize product work, synthesize interviews, and draft PRDs with practical PM frameworks.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network use, weak crypto, and sensitive file patterns, but review found these are mostly Markdown examples or product-management terms. The confirmed risks are local file reads and a fixed-name sample CSV write in CLI scripts.

low_findings.title: Unrestricted Local File Reads From CLI Arguments
low_findings.description: The interview analyzer reads the path supplied on the command line, and the RICE script reads a supplied CSV path. This is expected CLI behavior, but users could accidentally process sensitive local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are direct and visible in the scripts. Risk is limited because there is no network transmission or shell execution.

low_findings.title: Fixed Sample CSV Write Can Overwrite Local File
low_findings.description: The RICE sample command writes sample_features.csv in the current directory without checking for an existing file. This can overwrite local work if the command is run in a directory with that file name.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write path is fixed and the script does not check for an existing file. The behavior is intentional and local, so severity remains low.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Local File Input
dangerous_patterns.description: CLI scripts accept local file paths and read them directly. No evidence found of exfiltration, command injection, or prompt injection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The direct file reads are confirmed. Static network and external command findings were false positives from variable names and Markdown fences.

content.user_title: Plan Product Work with PM Frameworks
content.value_statement: Product teams need fast ways to turn research, requests, and strategy into clear decisions. This skill provides RICE scoring, interview synthesis, PRD templates, and discovery workflows.
actual_capabilities: Calculates and ranks feature ideas with the RICE framework.
Generates simple quarterly roadmap suggestions from scored features.
Extracts pain points, requests, themes, quotes, metrics, and competitors from interview text.
Provides standard PRD, one-page PRD, agile epic, and feature brief templates.
Documents discovery, prioritization, metrics, stakeholder, and go-to-market workflows.
Can output prioritization and interview analysis in text or JSON formats.
limitations: Interview analysis uses keyword and pattern matching, not advanced natural language models.
RICE scores depend on the quality of user-provided estimates.
The scripts read local files and do not validate that paths are inside a project folder.
Templates require human review before stakeholder approval or delivery planning.

use_cases.title: Rank a Feature Backlog
use_cases.description: Score competing feature ideas, compare effort, and create a first-pass quarterly roadmap.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Customer Interviews
use_cases.description: Extract pain points, feature requests, sentiment, themes, and useful quotes from research transcripts.

use_cases.title: Draft Product Requirements
use_cases.description: Choose a PRD format and structure problem, scope, requirements, metrics, risks, and launch plans.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize My Feature List
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the RICE framework to rank these features. Explain the top three priorities and any estimate concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an Interview Transcript
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this customer interview. Extract pain points, feature requests, jobs to be done, sentiment, themes, metrics, and key quotes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a One-Page PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-page PRD for this feature. Include problem, solution, why now, success metrics, scope, risks, timeline, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Discovery-to-Roadmap Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Combine discovery findings, RICE scores, dependencies, and capacity into a product plan with assumptions, tradeoffs, roadmap, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A CSV of ten candidate features with reach, impact, confidence, effort, and descriptions.
output: A ranked feature list with RICE scores.
A portfolio summary that separates quick wins from big bets.
A quarterly roadmap based on team capacity.

output_examples.input: A transcript from a customer interview about onboarding problems.
output: A sentiment summary with positive and negative signals.
A list of pain points, feature requests, jobs to be done, and key quotes.
Themes, metrics, and competitor mentions found in the transcript.

output_examples.input: A product idea that needs stakeholder review before engineering starts.
output: A structured PRD outline with problem, solution, scope, requirements, metrics, risks, and timeline.
Open questions that need answers before approval.

best_practices: Review RICE inputs with engineering, design, sales, and support before using the ranking for commitments.
Use interview outputs as research notes, then validate patterns across multiple customers.
Keep PRDs focused on problems, measurable outcomes, scope boundaries, risks, and acceptance criteria.
anti_patterns: Do not treat keyword-based interview analysis as a substitute for human research synthesis.
Do not use RICE scores without checking strategic fit, dependencies, and opportunity cost.
Do not run the scripts on files that may contain secrets or sensitive personal data unless required.

faq.question: What product management frameworks are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes RICE, value versus effort, MoSCoW, interview guides, hypothesis templates, and PRD formats.

faq.question: Can it create a roadmap?
faq.answer: Yes. The RICE script can suggest quarterly roadmap groupings based on ranked features and team capacity.

faq.question: Does interview analysis use an external service?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network calls. The script uses local keyword and pattern matching on provided text files.

faq.question: What input does the prioritization script need?
faq.answer: It expects feature rows with name, reach, impact, confidence, effort, and optional description fields.

faq.question: Are the PRD templates ready to publish as final documents?
faq.answer: No. They are starting points and need team-specific detail, stakeholder review, and delivery validation.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found low risk. Users should avoid processing sensitive local files with the included scripts.
